title: sindora javanica
intro: Sindora javanica, also known as Javan Sindora or Bintangor, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a large tree native to Southeast Asia, with medicinal properties attributed to its bark and leaves, which contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and is believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, offering potential benefits in reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and microbial infections. Sindora javanica is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment, and is also used in traditional medicine, highlighting its value as a valuable herbal remedy.
